hypotheses.statement: Operational interventions inside delegated authority, if separated, will execute within expected cycle times.
hypotheses.distinguishing_prediction: Cycle-time metrics at operational scale remain within prior variance.

hypotheses.statement: Out-of-scope interventions, if bundled with in-scope, will be executed without separate consent.
hypotheses.distinguishing_prediction: Consent records show no separate authorization for out-of-scope interventions.

hypotheses.statement: Scope-of-authority questioned downstream after bundled execution will be retroactively rationalized rather than refused.
hypotheses.distinguishing_prediction: Post-execution review records favor rationalization over refusal.
